mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with robust employment growth indicate synchronized demand expansion across durable goods and services sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate revenue forecasts rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns in high-housing/high-employment regimes do not exceed the cross-sectional median by at least 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply-side momentum, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50% during supply-constrained periods, or INDPRO increases coincide with negative SPX returns in > 40% of observations over a 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 3.5 percent threshold forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a positive predictive Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-inflation regimes are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings combined with expanding real GDP indicate robust consumer balance sheets and aggregate demand growth, which historically precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward test, or forward returns in high-savings/high-GDP regimes fail to outperform the cross-sectional median by at least 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals underlying economic resilience and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in payroll-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts in a high-interest-rate environment signal tightening financial conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns with a directional accuracy above 55% over a 3-year walk-forward test, or SPX forward returns in declining-housing regimes are not significantly lower than the cross-sectional median (p > 0.05).
